**MUTUAL FUND SCREENING: RISK-ADJUSTED OUTPERFORMERS**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
• Focus on actively managed equity mutual funds  
• Emphasis on consistency + quality of returns (not just absolute returns)  
• Based on robust multi-factor evaluation framework  

**SCREENING CRITERIA**  
• Minimum 3-year performance track record  
• Sharpe Ratio > 1 (efficient risk-return trade-off)  
• Sortino Ratio > 1 (better downside risk management)  
• Positive Information Ratio (consistent benchmark outperformance)  
• Alpha > 1 (genuine excess return generation)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
• Filters out funds with volatile or unsustainable performance  
• Identifies process-driven fund managers with disciplined strategy  
• Focus shifts from returns → risk-adjusted wealth creation  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
• Positive (for long-term portfolio construction)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
• This framework highlights high-quality mutual funds that deliver consistent, risk-efficient returns—ideal for building long-term, stable wealth rather than chasing short-term performance

Polycab Management Meet Takeaways:

Q4 margins at the lower end of guidance

Expects flat Q4 cable & wire (C&W) volume growth

Domestic demand slowdown is timing-related

Exports face near-term headwinds from the Middle East

India’s commodity derivatives market has gone from ₹151 trillion in FY23 to ₹958 trillion in just 9M FY26.

When a market grows 6x, and one platform captures nearly all of it, that is worth paying attention to.

Here’s what’s happening 🧵

https://x.com/ionicwealth/status/2036313768100241868?s=20

**POWER GRID: MOSL VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Neutral  
- Target Price: ₹302  
- Capex and capitalization guidance revised upward for FY26  

**CAPEX & GUIDANCE UPDATE**  
- FY26 Capex: ₹350 bn (↑ from ₹320 bn; vs earlier ₹280 bn)  
- FY26 Capitalization: ₹250 bn (↑ from ₹220 bn; vs earlier ₹200 bn)  
- FY27/28 Guidance unchanged:  
  • FY27: ₹300 bn / ₹350 bn  
  • FY28: ₹370 bn / ₹450 bn  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Capex based on current order visibility; further wins may drive upgrades  
- Strong multi-trillion transmission opportunity unfolding in India  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Improved execution (ROW resolution) supporting higher capitalization  
- Large pipeline ensures long-term growth visibility  
- Incremental upside dependent on new project awards  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Balanced risk-reward at current levels  
- Strong fundamentals but limited near-term rerating triggers  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid remains a **high-visibility utility growth story**, with capex momentum improving, but near-term upside depends on fresh order inflows and valuation comfort

**POWER GRID: HSBC VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Hold  
- Target Price: ₹290  
- Capex & capitalization guidance raised; scope for further upgrades  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Large transmission TAM of ~₹15 tn over next decade  
- Opportunity likely back-ended (execution skewed to later years)  

**NEW OPPORTUNITIES**  
- Intra-state transmission (intra-STS) expansion  
- BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems) emerging as new growth avenue  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong long-term visibility driven by energy transition  
- Additional optionality from storage and intra-state projects  
- However, near-term growth may be moderate due to back-ended pipeline  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Limited upside at current levels  
- Balanced risk-reward despite improving capex outlook  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid remains a **steady long-term infrastructure play**, with new opportunities emerging, but near-term upside constrained due to valuation and timing of growth execution

**POWER GRID: KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Reduce  
- Target Price: ₹300  
- Valuations seen as stretched despite strong growth visibility  

**CAPEX & GUIDANCE UPDATE**  
- FY26 Capex: ₹350 bn (↑ from ₹320 bn)  
- FY26 Capitalization: ₹250 bn (↑ from ₹220 bn)  
- Improvement driven by resolution of ROW (Right of Way) issues  
- FY27–28 guidance maintained  

**LONG-TERM OPPORTUNITY**  
- CEA estimates ~₹7.9 tn transmission capex for non-fossil capacity till FY36  
- Additional opportunities:  
  • Undersea projects: ~₹3 tn  
  • Brahmaputra basin: ~₹4 tn  
- Targeting ₹400 bn capex / ₹350 bn capitalization annually (FY29–36)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong, visible long-term growth runway  
- Beneficiary of renewable energy transition and grid expansion  
- Execution improving with ROW bottlenecks easing  

**VALUATION CONCERN**  
- Trading at ~16x P/E → considered full valuation  
- Limited upside despite strong fundamentals  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid offers **high visibility, stable growth**, but elevated valuations cap near-term upside, leading to a cautious stance despite strong structural tailwinds

**COAL INDIA: NUVAMA VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Reduce  
- Target Price: ₹384  
- Growth narrative seen weakening amid supply-demand mismatch  

**DEMAND & PRICING OUTLOOK**  
- Excess domestic supply + rising competition limiting pricing power  
- E-auction premium expected stable at ~35–40% (vs ~37% avg in 9MFY26)  
- Prices likely range-bound despite higher global coal prices  

**VOLUME OUTLOOK**  
- No meaningful volume growth visibility in FY26  
- ~4% volume CAGR (FY26–28E) at risk  
- Increasing supply from captive miners impacting growth  

**MARGIN & COST PRESSURES**  
- Upcoming wage revision (July 2026) → cost escalation  
- Limited ability to pass on higher costs  
- Earnings growth expected to remain muted  

**EARNINGS OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA CAGR: ~4% (FY26–28E)  
- Reflects subdued growth and margin pressures  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Structural challenges from rising domestic supply  
- Pricing power constrained despite global tailwinds  
- Cost inflation (wages) adds further pressure  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Coal India faces a **low-growth, margin-constrained phase**, with limited upside as supply pressures and cost headwinds outweigh pricing benefits

**BAJAJ FINANCE: MOSL VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Buy  
- Target Price: ₹900  
- Near-term growth reset, but medium-term acceleration intact  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Short-term moderation in growth  
- Strong medium-term trajectory driven by scale and product expansion  

**TECH & OPERATING EDGE**  
- AI embedded as a core operating layer  
- Enhances underwriting precision, productivity, and scalability  
- Structural advantage in customer acquisition & risk management  

**RISK & ASSET QUALITY**  
- Transition from reactive clean-up to proactive risk architecture  
- Strengthening credit filters and portfolio quality  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Trading at ~3.7x FY27E P/BV and ~20x P/E  
- Valuations reasonable but not deeply attractive given near-term uncertainties  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong franchise with tech-led differentiation  
- Earnings growth visibility intact over medium term  
- Near-term caution due to growth reset and valuation comfort  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive (medium-term), Neutral (near-term)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Bajaj Finance remains a **high-quality compounding NBFC**, where temporary growth moderation is a reset rather than a structural concern, with AI-led capabilities supporting long-term dominance

**LIC HOUSING FINANCE: CITI VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Buy  
- Target Price: ₹730  
- Attractive valuation at ~0.55x FY27 book  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- AUM growth recovering from ~5% YoY in 3Q  
- FY26 exit growth expected >6%  
- 4Q disbursements likely to see double-digit growth  
- Lower balance transfer (BT) pressure aiding growth  

**BUSINESS & PRICING STRATEGY**  
- ₹365 bn loan book repriced since Oct → reduces BT-out  
- Competitive home loan rates (~7.15%) maintained  
- Focus on NIM stability via:  
  • Mix shift toward non-home loans  
  • Lower funding costs  

**ASSET QUALITY & BALANCE SHEET**  
- Retail book stable  
- Corporate resolutions may provide provisioning upside  
- GS3 (Stage 3 assets) expected to normalize by FY27  

**STRATEGIC INITIATIVES**  
- Organizational restructuring with external consultants  
- Distribution reset + co-lending model from FY27  
- Fintech partnerships to drive incremental growth  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Growth revival + margin protection combination  
- Asset quality improving with potential write-back upside  
- Multiple levers (distribution + fintech) for medium-term growth  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- LIC Housing offers a **value + recovery play**, with improving growth momentum, stable margins, and attractive valuation providing strong risk-reward

**POWER GRID: ELARA CAPITAL VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Buy  
- Target Price: ₹360  
- Strong capex pipeline and transmission opportunity visibility  

**CAPEX & GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- FY26 Capex: ₹350 bn (↑ from ₹320 bn)  
- FY26 Capitalization: ₹250 bn (↑ from ₹220 bn)  
- FY27 Capex Target: ₹370 bn  
- FY28 Capex Target: ₹450 bn  

**SECTOR OPPORTUNITY**  
- Transmission opportunity: ~₹15 tn till FY36  
- Order Book: ~₹2.15 tn  
- CWIP (Work-in-progress): ~₹490 bn  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong capex visibility ensures steady asset addition and regulated returns  
- Large order book + CWIP pipeline supports future revenue growth  
- Beneficiary of India’s power demand growth and renewable integration  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Power Grid remains a **stable compounding utility play**, with strong visibility on growth driven by a massive transmission capex cycle and regulated return framework

**GMR AIRPORTS: KOTAK INSTITUTIONAL EQUITIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Buy  
- Target Price: ₹112  
- Current Price: ~₹85 → attractive upside potential  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Relatively insulated from key external risks:  
  • Middle East conflict  
  • Airport tariff resets (control periods)  
  • Competition from Jewar airport  

**VALUATION & UPSIDE**  
- Trading at ~10x FY30E EV/EBITDA  
- Current valuation does not factor upside from pending legal cases  
- Strong positioning in legal outcomes provides optionality  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Stable airport business with predictable cash flows  
- Limited near-term regulatory and competitive risks  
- Hidden value from legal resolutions not priced in  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- GMR Airports offers a **low-risk, optionality-driven play**, where downside is limited by stable operations and upside can emerge from favorable legal outcomes and rerating

**TITAN: CLSA VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: Outperform  
- Target Price: ₹4,996 (raised from ₹4,674)  
- Strong expected 4QFY26 performance led by jewellery  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- 4QFY26 Revenue Growth: ~43% YoY  
  • Jewellery: ~57.6% YoY (ex-bullion)  
  • Watches: ~12.1% YoY  
  • Eyewear: ~16.9% YoY  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- Jewellery EBIT margin expected at ~10.4%  
  • Down vs 10.6% (3QFY26)  
  • Down vs 11.9% (4QFY25)  
- Margin pressure due to higher discounts to drive volumes  

**EARNINGS REVISION**  
- EPS estimates raised ~5–6% for FY26–FY28  
- Driven by stronger-than-expected sales growth  
- Partially offset by lower jewellery margins  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Volume-led growth strategy gaining traction  
- Market share gains prioritized over margins  
- Strong brand + festive/wedding demand tailwind  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Titan continues to deliver strong growth momentum, with aggressive volume push supporting earnings upgrades despite near-term margin moderation
BofA maintains 'Buy' ratings on Marico with a target price of Rs 860.

**OIL & GAS SECTOR: GOLDMAN SACHS VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Risk-reward for OMCs turns less attractive  
- Downgrades across OMC space amid margin concerns  
- Divergent stance: Bullish on RIL, Bearish on ONGC  

**OMCs – DOWNGRADE RATIONALE**  
- Marketing margins negatively correlated with crude prices  
- Limited ability to pass on higher crude/product prices to consumers  
- Margin pressure likely if oil prices rise  

**RATING CHANGES**  
- HPCL: Downgraded to Neutral | TP ₹310 (cut)  
- BPCL: Downgraded to Neutral | TP ₹340 (cut)  
- IOC: Downgraded to Sell | TP ₹110 (cut)  

**OTHER CALLS**  
- Reliance Industries: Buy | TP raised to ₹1,910  
- ONGC: Sell | TP ₹245  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- OMCs benefit from lower crude but face pressure when oil rises  
- Pricing controls limit margin flexibility → earnings volatility  
- Integrated players like RIL better positioned due to diversified earnings  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative for OMCs  
- Positive for RIL  
- Negative for ONGC (as per GS view)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Goldman Sachs turns cautious on OMCs due to structural margin constraints, favoring integrated energy plays while remaining selective in upstream exposure

**ONGC: CLSA HIGH CONVICTION CALL**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Rating: High Conviction Outperform  
- Target Price: ₹415 (raised)  
- Stock underperformed globally due to windfall tax fears  

**KEY RE-RATING DRIVERS**  
- New law limits government’s ability to impose windfall tax  
- Market still pricing conservative Brent ~US$63/bbl  
- Upside scenario: US$90/bbl → ~65% potential upside  

**PRODUCTION & PRICE TRIGGERS**  
- Gas production expected to rise ~15% in next 3–4 months  
- New well gas prices likely to jump >50%  
- Volume + pricing tailwind for earnings  

**EARNINGS UPGRADE**  
- Brent assumptions revised to:  
  • FY27: US$89/bbl  
  • FY28: US$82/bbl  
- EPS impact:  
  • FY27: ↑ ~34%  
  • FY28: ↑ ~14%  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Market overhang (windfall tax fear) may be overdone  
- Earnings highly leveraged to crude & gas price cycle  
- Near-term catalysts: production ramp-up + pricing reset  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Strong Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- ONGC presents a **high-beta play on crude prices with policy overhang easing**, where improving production and gas pricing can drive sharp earnings upgrades and potential re-rating

**POLYCAB: MORGAN STANLEY VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price revised to ₹8,707 (from ₹9,659)  
- Rating maintained at Overweight  
- EPS estimates cut ~5% for FY26–FY28  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Cable & Wire (C&W) revenue cut by ~4–5%  
- Still expects strong ~20% CAGR in C&W (FY25–FY28)  
- FMEG revenue estimates reduced by ~2–3%  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- FY26: ~13.4%  
- FY27: ~13.2%  
- FY28: ~13.0%  
- Indicates gradual margin normalization  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Core growth story intact despite near-term estimate cuts  
- Strong demand outlook in cables & wires driven by infra, capex cycle  
- Margins likely to soften slightly due to competitive intensity / mix  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive (with near-term moderation)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Polycab remains a structural growth story in cables & wires, with strong revenue visibility, though earnings and margin expectations have been slightly tempered in the near term

NUVAMA ON DEFENCE SECTOR

Remain structurally positive; BEL top pick

Modern warfare shifting toward drones, Al, electronic warfare and directed energy weapons.

India accelerating indigenisation with strong policy push

Aatmanirbhar Bharat acting as a key demand driver with large domestic procurement pipeline and rising export ambitions

Defence electronics emerging as a key segment with focus on IP-led, high-tech capabilities and lower import dependence

Data Patterns and Solar Industries for medium-term growth

HAL offers limited downside with execution recovery as key trigger

**FRACTAL ANALYTICS: GOLDMAN SACHS VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Initiates with Neutral rating  
- Target Price: ₹910  
- Large analytics TAM opportunity (~$328 bn)  

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Revenue CAGR: ~20% (FY26–FY30)  
- EBIT CAGR: ~27%  
- Strong exposure to AI/ML and advanced analytics segments  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Trades at ~41x FY27 P/E  
- Premium valuation already pricing in strong growth expectations  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Positioned well in high-growth analytics and AI space  
- Strong earnings growth visibility, especially at EBIT level  
- However, upside limited due to already rich valuations  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Fractal Analytics offers strong structural growth in AI/analytics, but premium valuation caps near-term upside, leading to a balanced risk-reward outlook

**FRACTAL ANALYTICS: MORGAN STANLEY INITIATION**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Initiates with Overweight rating  
- Target Price: ₹964  
- Positioned as a high-growth challenger in tech services  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Strong positioning in data, analytics, and AI-led services  
- Platform-centric model enabling agentic AI delivery  
- Revenue growth expected to outpace industry peers  

**MARGIN & VALUATION VIEW**  
- High gross margins indicate quality business model  
- EBIT margin gap vs peers expected to narrow over time  
- Trades at premium P/E, justified by superior growth outlook  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Structural shift toward AI & analytics favors Fractal’s capabilities  
- Platform-led approach provides scalability and differentiation  
- Margin expansion is key monitorable for re-rating  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Fractal Analytics offers a compelling growth + AI play, with premium valuation supported by strong positioning and expected margin expansion trajectory

**APOLLO HOSPITALS: CITI VIEW UPDATE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹9,600  
- Recommendation: Buy (maintained)  
- Added to Citi’s Pan-Asia Focus List  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Preferred pick in Indian hospital space  
- Structurally resilient core hospital business  
- Fast-growing pharmacy segment with digital profitability in sight  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Hospitals provide stable, high-margin cash flows  
- Pharmacy + digital ecosystem offers scalable growth opportunity  
- Valuations below long-term averages (absolute & relative)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Apollo Hospitals offers a strong combination of defensive healthcare earnings and high-growth optionality, with valuation comfort supporting upside potential

**INDIGO: JEFFERIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹6,140  
- Recommendation: Buy  
- Summer schedule provides improved visibility on capacity growth  

**CAPACITY & GROWTH OUTLOOK**  
- Domestic departures expected to grow ~6–7% YoY  
- Reported schedules slightly lower YoY, indicating calibrated expansion  
- Supports FY27 ASK growth estimate of ~10–11%  
- Still below historical ASK CAGR of ~13–15%  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Controlled capacity growth reduces execution and oversupply risks  
- Improved confidence in demand-supply balance  
- Earnings visibility now linked to fuel cost trajectory  

**KEY RISK**  
- Prolonged West Asia crisis → higher ATF (jet fuel) prices  
- Fuel inflation could pressure margins despite stable capacity growth  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive (with fuel cost sensitivity)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- IndiGo’s disciplined capacity growth strengthens earnings visibility, but profitability remains highly sensitive to fuel prices amid ongoing geopolitical risks

**ETERNAL: KOTAK SECURITIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹375  
- Recommendation: Buy  
- Platform fee hike implemented to counter rising costs  

**KEY DRIVERS**  
- Higher packaging costs impacting unit economics  
- Increase in average delivery distance per order  
- Potential petrol price hikes adding to cost pressure  
- Restaurants limiting menu → expansion of delivery radius  

**COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**  
- Swiggy yet to implement similar fee hike (may follow)  
- Industry may move towards higher pricing to sustain margins  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Platform fee hike expected to largely offset cost inflation  
- Helps maintain contribution margin (CM) at segment level  
- Structural shift towards higher delivery radius may increase cost base  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Timely pricing action positions Eternal to defend margins amid rising delivery and input costs, with potential industry-wide pricing normalization ahead

**BANKING SECTOR: NOMURA VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Kotak Mahindra Bank upgraded to Buy; Target Price ₹445  
- System credit growth accelerated to ~14.9% YoY (vs ~10% earlier)  
- Sector facing rising funding costs and liquidity tightness  

**SECTOR TRENDS**  
- Credit-Deposit (CD) Ratio elevated at ~82% vs ~75% historical average  
- Liquidity buffers & LCRs declining across banks  
- Deposit mobilisation lagging credit growth  
- Government spending & FX inflows remain weak  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
- Funding costs rising despite policy easing  
- NIM estimates revised downward across the sector  
- Profitability may face near-term pressure  

**STOCK PREFERENCES**  
- Top Picks: Kotak Mahindra Bank, Axis Bank, ICICI Bank  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Strong credit growth is a positive, but sustainability depends on deposit growth  
- Higher CD ratio signals tighter liquidity → competition for deposits likely to intensify  
- Margin compression risk persists due to rising cost of funds  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral (Growth strong, but margins under pressure)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Banking sector is in a high-growth but tightening liquidity phase, where deposit mobilisation and cost of funds will be the key drivers for earnings going forward

**ASIAN PAINTS: MORGAN STANLEY VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹2,126  
- Recommendation: Underweight  
- Price hikes of ~6–8% expected in two phases  

**KEY OBSERVATIONS**  
- Company aiming to minimize price hikes to protect demand  
- Customer sensitivity influencing pricing strategy  
- Thinners likely to see sharper price increases  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Calibrated price hikes indicate demand remains fragile  
- Limited pricing aggression may delay margin recovery  
- Product-level pricing (like thinners) used to partially offset cost pressures  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Despite planned price hikes, weak demand and cautious pricing approach may keep margin recovery slow, supporting a cautious stance on the stock

**ASIAN PAINTS: MACQUARIE VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹3,100  
- Recommendation: Outperform  
- Announces 6–8% price hike in two phases  

**KEY DRIVERS**  
- Strong pricing power across the industry  
- Price hikes aimed at passing on input cost inflation  
- Follows historical pattern of gradual price transmission  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Confirms ability of paint companies to protect margins via pricing  
- Input cost-led margin pressure likely temporary  
- Phased hikes reduce demand disruption risk  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Asian Paints’ pricing strength reinforces its leadership position, with margin headwinds seen as short-term and recoverable through calibrated price hikes

**APOLLO HOSPITALS: CITI VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price: ₹9,600  
- Recommendation: Buy  
- Added to Citi’s Pan-Asia focus list  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
- Preferred pick in Indian hospital space  
- Structurally resilient core hospital business  
- Strong growth in pharmacy segment with digital profitability in sight  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Hospitals business provides stable cash flows and pricing power  
- Pharmacy + digital ecosystem offers long-term growth optionality  
- Valuations below long-term averages (both absolute & relative)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Apollo Hospitals combines defensive healthcare cash flows with high-growth pharmacy/digital play, making it a strong long-term compounder at reasonable valuations

**PAINT SECTOR: MORGAN STANLEY VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Demand remains weak, especially in retail segment  
- Price hikes expected across players to support margins  
- Competitive intensity rising with higher discounts and incentives  

**PRICING & DEMAND TRENDS**  
- Asian Paints: No price hike until Mar 20; hike expected in early April  
- Berger Paints: Already implemented ~4–5% hikes in select solvent-based products (from Mar 21)  
- Industry: Gradual price hikes likely rather than sharp increases  

**COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**  
- Increasing aggression from JSW Paints & Akzo Nobel vs earlier periods  
- Higher dealer & painter incentives impacting margins  
- Asian Paints intensifying efforts to regain retail share (especially West India)  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Weak demand + rising competition = pressure on volume growth  
- Price hikes may support margins but risk further demand slowdown  
- Elevated discounting suggests stress in maintaining market share  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Slightly Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Paint sector faces a tough near-term setup with weak demand and rising competition, where margin recovery depends on successful price hikes without hurting volumes

**CHEMICAL SECTOR: JEFFERIES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Middle East conflict disrupting chemical feedstock supply chains  
- India heavily dependent on the region for key raw materials  
- Near-term inventory gains, but margin pressure ahead  

**SECTORAL IMPACT**  
- Most Impacted: Fertilizers  
- Other Affected: Plastics, Paints, Adhesives, Explosives, Consumer Durables  
- Refgas: Sharp input cost inflation → margin pressure  
- Crop Protection: Better positioned due to cost pass-through in export contracts  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Q4 (March quarter): Likely inventory gains due to rising prices  
- From April onwards:  
  • Operating rate cuts possible  
  • Margin compression if supply disruptions persist  
- Companies with pricing power or export-linked contracts relatively safer  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative (especially from Q1 FY27 onwards if disruption sustains)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Short-term gains may mask underlying stress, but prolonged conflict can trigger margin compression and production cuts across the chemical value chain

**ITC: GOLDMAN SACHS VIEW UPDATE**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Target Price cut to ₹330 (from ₹385)  
- Rating maintained at Neutral  
- Earnings estimates downgraded  

**KEY REASONS**  
- Calibrated price hikes instead of sharp increase to offset tax impact  
- Higher-than-expected pressure on cigarette margins  
- Input cost inflation impacting FMCG margins  

**STRATEGIC INSIGHT**  
- Gradual pricing strategy may protect volumes but delays margin recovery  
- Cigarette business (core profit driver) facing near-term profitability pressure  
- FMCG margin expansion outlook remains challenging  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Slightly Negative  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- ITC faces near-term earnings pressure due to tax and cost headwinds, with limited upside until margin recovery visibility improves

UBS On ITC

Target Price ₹395

Recommendation Buy 

New prices should protect volumes

Adopted a three-pronged pricing strategy to protect volumes

ITC has ensured a same-price option across all sensitive variants

This should largely limit volume impact

Nonetheless, ITC is likely to exceed the bearish consensus expectations for FY27

Valuation is very appealing in our view

**ASK AUTOMOTIVE: CLSA MAINTAINS OUTPERFORM**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Outperform maintained  
- Target Price: ₹640  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Honda (HMSI) expanding capacity ~28% (6.25mn → 8mn units)  
- ₹1,500 Cr capex at Tapukara plant  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- ASK derives ~35% revenue from HMSI → strong beneficiary  
- Expansion supports higher component demand  
- EV transition tailwind: 30–50% higher content per vehicle vs ICE  

**OUTLOOK**  
- HMSI product push strengthens long-term growth visibility  
- ASK well positioned for next growth phase driven by EV + volume expansion  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- HMSI capacity expansion and EV shift create a strong structural growth tailwind for ASK Automotive

**ALLIED BLENDERS: NUVAMA MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹670  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Q3 weakness due to regulatory factors (Telangana destocking, Maharashtra policy)  
- Q4 expected to see recovery  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Premiumisation trend continues; ~50% mix by FY28 (vs 47% currently)  
- ABD Maestro (JV with Ranveer Singh) gaining traction with new launches  
- Focus on luxury positioning through premium portfolio  

**MARGINS & OUTLOOK**  
- EBITDA margin expected to expand to ~17–18% by FY28 (vs 13.2%)  
- Supported by favourable raw material basket, India-UK FTA & backward integration  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong premiumisation strategy and margin expansion visibility support long-term growth despite near-term regulatory disruptions

**KALYAN JEWELLERS: MOSL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹550  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong demand in India despite rising gold prices  
- Near-term impact expected on MENA business due to geopolitical issues  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Continued aggressive store expansion in India  
- Candere (online segment) turns profitable with strong momentum  

**MARGINS & FINANCIALS**  
- EBITDA margin expansion driven by better mix & operating leverage  
- Deleveraging and lower finance costs to support profitability  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong domestic demand and improving profitability drivers outweigh near-term geopolitical risks

**STAR HEALTH: JEFFERIES MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹660  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Loss ratio expected to improve (~200 bps over FY25–28E)  
- Driven by resumed price hikes & selective underwriting  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Additional levers: claim automation, hospital partnerships, Public Insurance Registry  
- Strong earnings visibility with ~21% IFRS EPS CAGR (FY26–28E)  

**VALUATION**  
- Trading at ~18x FY27E P/E; does not fully reflect growth potential  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Improving profitability metrics and strong growth outlook make current valuations attractive

**PREMIER ENERGIES: DAM CAPITAL MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹1,295  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Strong competitive moat driven by technology & execution capabilities  
- Superior positioning in solar cell manufacturing  

**COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE**  
- Best-in-class cell efficiency  
- Ability to commission & stabilise plants without tech tie-ups  
- Faster ramp-up (4–6 months vs 12–18 months for peers)  
- Strong in-house R&D (G12R cells, zero busbar cells)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Technological edge and execution strength support long-term growth  
- Difficult for peers to replicate capabilities  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong execution + innovation-led moat positions Premier Energies as a key beneficiary in solar manufacturing growth

reports.text:
**HDFC BANK: HSBC MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price cut to ₹990 (from ₹1,070)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Chairman resignation seen as difference of opinion, not governance concern  
- However, may lead to valuation multiple compression  

**VALUATION VIEW**  
- Core bank valued at ~2.2x FY27E BVPS (vs 2.4x earlier)  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Improvement in operating performance required to offset sentiment impact  
- Fundamentals remain stable  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Leadership change impacts near-term valuations, but long-term story intact subject to performance delivery

**HSBC: FINANCIALS SECTOR VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Concerns over sustained high oil prices impacting growth, inflation & rates  
- Potential pressure on asset quality if macro stress persists  

**KEY DEBATES**  
- PSU vs Private banks positioning  
- Risk of asset quality deterioration  
- Strategy: Position for rate cuts vs prolonged high-rate environment

**DAM Capital Note – Key Highlights (Macro & Sector)**

**Macro & Commodities (Strait of Hormuz shock)**  
- Closure hits **~20% of global LNG seaborne trade**, **~32% of LPG seaborne exports** and **~10% of seaborne oil**  
- India is highly dependent on **Strait of Hormuz (SOH)** for **LNG, LPG and oil**  
- For India: each **1 USD/bbl above budgeted 70 USD/bbl** adds **~₹100bn to fiscal deficit**  
- Sustained **85–90+ USD/bbl crude** can push **CAD to 1.5–2% of GDP in Q1 FY27**, **CPI >4.5%** and **INR toward 95**  

**Oil Availability vs Product Bottlenecks**  
- **Crude and transport fuels (gasoline, diesel, ATF)** are “manageable” via **inventories, IEA release and Russian supply**  
- Real bottlenecks: **refined products, LNG, LPG, NGLs, methanol, sulphur, helium and aluminium** with **sharp price risk**  

**India – Availability & Pricing**  
- Roughly **okay on crude and transport fuels** thanks to domestic refining and diversified sourcing; **LPG and bitumen** most stressed  
  - LPG inventory ~15 days  
  - Long lead times from US  
- **Petrol/diesel pass‑through politically constrained** (election‑heavy year):  
  - OMCs initially absorb **marketing losses**  
  - **Excise cuts likely** if crude stays elevated  
- **External‑sector headwinds**:  
  - Higher **freight/insurance (potential 2x)**  
  - Longer routes via **Cape of Good Hope**  
  - Around **38% of India’s remittances** at risk from Gulf disruptions  

**Big Macro Conclusions**  
- **Near term**: macro fundamentals hold; stress appears first in **CAD, INR and subsidies** (oil, fertiliser)  
- **If war lasts >1 quarter with crude >100 USD/bbl**:  
  - **Inflation risk (CPI >4.5%)**  
  - **Pause in rate cuts**  
  - **Fiscal deficit 10–20 bps wider**  
  - **GDP growth downgrade (FY27 to ~6.8% vs 7%)**  

**Sector Winners (India – relative positives)**  
- **Upstream O&G (ONGC, Oil India)**:  
  - **~11–12% EBITDA uplift per 5 USD/bbl**  
  - Lower perceived **windfall‑tax risk** under ORDA framework  
- **RIL (Refining, petchem)**:  
  - **~2.5% EBITDA boost per 1 USD/bbl GRM gain**  
  - Spot GRMs >25 USD/bbl vs normal ~8 USD/bbl; petchem also improving  
- **Sugar**:  
  - Higher crude → **more Brazilian ethanol diversion** → **tighter global sugar**  
  - Better **prices and export opportunity** (0.6–1 MT vs 0.3 MT already exported)  
  - **India ethanol‑blending push** may accelerate  
- **Select agro‑input names** (fertilisers, sulphur‑linked) benefit from **inventory gains** and **better pricing** if disruption persists  

**Sector Losers (India – clear negatives)**  
- **OMCs (IOCL, BPCL, HPCL) & CGDs (IGL, MGL, GUJGAS)**:  
  - **~30–50% EBITDA hit per 5 USD/bbl** (no retail price hike)  
  - **CGDs**: lose **~₹2/scm of EBITDA (15–30%) without pass‑through**  
- **Airlines**:  
  - **ATF is 35–40% of cost**; **10% ATF rise cuts EBITDA ~15%**  
  - Also hit by **ME airspace closures and rerouting of long‑haul**  
- **Infra**:  
  - Higher oil squeezes fiscal space and **Middle East order flows**  
  - Risk to India’s **>₹12tn capex plan** if crude averages ~90 USD/bbl (~₹2tn fiscal hit)  
- **Cement**:  
  - No ME fuel sourcing, but **10% rise in petcoke/coal cuts EBITDA/t by ~8% (~₹130/t)**  
  - Polypropylene bag costs add another **~₹80/t**  
- **Tiles & bathware / wood panels**:  
  - Gas and resin cost inflation → **70–150 bps margin risk** for tiles, **30–60 bps** for wood panels on 10% energy/RM rise  
  - Added **volume pressure** from weak demand  

**Mixed / Time‑Dependent Impacts (India)**  
- **Fertilisers**:  
  - Near‑term **input‑cost/logistics shock**; not an immediate availability crisis  
  - **DAP** most exposed via imports, **urea** on gas/curtailment  
  - **SSP and NPK** better placed but face **sulphur/ammonia/phosphate cost risk**  
- **Chemicals**:  
  - Near‑term **inventory gains**, then **refilling at higher RM and freight costs**  
  - Most exposed chains: **methanol, ammonia, crude‑linked petrochemicals** (ethylene, propylene, benzene, toluene)  
- **FMCG**:  
  - **Crude‑linked packaging (PET, HDPE, laminates

  - Laurus Bio fermentation  
  - FDF Unit‑2  
  - Steroids & hormones  
  - KRKA JV (3bn OSD + 100mn potent tablets in Phase‑1)  

**Financials (FY24–28E)**  
- **Net sales**: ₹50.0bn → ₹95.7bn  
- **EBITDA margin**: 15.5% → 26.9%  
- **RoE**: 3.9% → 19.9%  
- **RoCE**: 6.2% → 20.7%  
- **EPS**: ~₹3.0 (FY24) → ₹25.0 (FY28) (**CAGR >40%**)  
- **Net‑debt‑to‑equity**: 0.6x → 0.2x  

**Segment Mix (9MFY26 & FY28E)**  
- **9MFY26 sales mix**:  
  - **CDMO**: 29.8% (small‑molecule 27.4%, Bio 2.4%)  
  - **ARV**: 27.6%  
  - **Oncology/other APIs + FDF**: 42.6%  
- **Modelled FY28 CDMO share**: 41%  

**ARV & Bio Details**  
- **ARV pricing**: No erosion in last 12 months  
- Volumes supported by **leading dolutegravir position** and **stable lamivudine/tenofovir demand**  
- **Lenacapavir**: Seen as **limited near‑term threat** due to higher cost and gradual EM rollout  
- **WHO regimen change** not expected before ~2029

**Laurus Labs – Motilal Oswal Key Data (19 Mar 2026)**

- **Rating**: BUY  
- **CMP**: ~₹945  
- **TP**: ₹1,280  
- **Upside**: ~35%  
- **12‑month forward P/E**: 62x  
- **EPS CAGR (FY26–28)**: ~16%  

**Growth Assumptions (FY25–28E)**  
- **Sales**: ₹68.4bn → ₹88.3bn (CAGR ~17%)  
- **EBITDA**: ₹17.7bn → ₹23.2bn (CAGR ~38%)  
- **EBITDA margin**: 19.0% → 26.3%  
- **PAT**: ₹3.1bn → ₹11.5bn  
- **EPS**: ₹5.8 → ₹21.3  
- **PAT growth path**: FY26E +174%; FY27E +11.9%; FY28E +19.8%  
- **RoE**: 7.2% → 17.4%  
- **RoCE**: 6.7% → 13.8%  
- **Net D/E**: 0.4 → 0.3 over FY26–28E  
- **Annual capex (FY26–28E)**: ~₹9–10bn  

**Multiples (FY26–28E)**  
- **P/E**: 59.7x / 53.4x / 44.5x  
- **EV/EBITDA**: 30.4x / 27.0x / 23.2x  

**CDMO & Business Metrics**  
- **CDMO revenue growth over last decade**: ~69x  
- **Non‑ARV revenue (9MFY26) YoY growth**: ~84%  

**Segment & Capex Markers**  
- **Biologics / animal‑health & crop‑science**:  
  - Capex: ~₹7–7.5bn  
  - Current revenue: ~₹1.5bn  
  - Potential long‑run revenue: >₹10bn  
- **Spray‑drying set‑up**: Customer‑funded, CDMO‑oriented  
- **KRKA JV capacity (mid‑CY27)**: 3bn tablets + 100mn potent‑solid units

**HDFC Bank – Important Key Highlights**

**Event & Governance Overhang**  
- **Sudden resignation** of part‑time Chairman & Independent Director **Atanu Chakraborty** on **18 March 2026**, citing lack of **congruence between certain bank practices and his personal ethics**  
- Management and board state **no regulatory or compliance lapses**; **RBI quickly approved Keki Mistry as interim part‑time Chairman for one quarter**, framed as a **signal of regulatory confidence**  

**Management Continuity & Governance**  
- CEO **Sashidhar Jagdishan** denies any **power struggle**; **Deputy MD Kaizad Bharucha** re‑appointed for **three years** with **expanded responsibilities**  
- Board highlights **robust governance** with active **audit, risk and ESG committees**, **structured whistle‑blower / social‑media complaint channels**, and **historically unanimous voting** on major resolutions during Chakraborty’s tenure  

**Investment View & Valuation**  
- Street expects a **near‑term “governance discount”** until a **permanent Chairman** is appointed and **MD & CEO re‑appointment** is cleared in coming months  
- Core **bank valuation multiple cut slightly** from **~2.3x to ~2x FY28E book value**; with **sub‑sidiaries valued at ~₹125/share**, this implies a **revised TP of ~₹1,025** and **BUY** rating vs **CMP ~₹800 (c. 28% upside)**  

**Financial Health & Projections**  
- **Core fundamentals seen as intact**:  
  - **NII** expected to grow from **₹1.09tn in FY24 to ₹1.72tn by FY28**  
  - **Net profit** from **₹608bn to ₹986bn** over the same period  
- **Return ratios healthy**:  
  - **RoA 1.8–2.0%**  
  - **RoE 14–17%**  
- **P/B de‑rates from ~2.3x today to ~1.5x on FY28E** even as earnings continue to compound  

If you like, I can now give a **short, all‑bullet‑only “thesis + key risks”** section explicitly around **trading this governance event**.

**L&T: MACQUARIE MAINTAINS OUTPERFORM**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Outperform maintained  
- Target Price: ₹4,910  

**OPERATIONS UPDATE**  
- ~95% project sites operating normally  
- ~5% sites stalled due to risk proximity (military zones)  
- Stalled sites have minimal revenue contribution  

**KEY RISKS**  
- Logistics & supply chain disruptions flagged  
- Revenue deferment risk if situation extends beyond ~3 months (site inventory buffer)  

**VALUATION & OUTLOOK**  
- Stock corrected ~22% in last month  
- Macquarie sees strong value at current levels  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Limited operational disruption but supply risks persist; sharp correction offers attractive entry opportunity

**GRASIM: MORGAN STANLEY MAINTAINS OVERWEIGHT**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Overweight maintained  
- Target Price: ₹385 (unchanged)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Paints business targeting ₹10,000 Cr revenue by 2028  
- Dealer network expansion to ~50,000  
- Strong volume growth; pricing impacted due to discounting  

**STRATEGIC DRIVERS**  
- Margin upside from premiumisation & scale benefits  
- B2B & B2C e-commerce expected to breakeven by FY27  
- Cellulose capacity expansion (~10kt) underway  
- Shift towards specialty chemicals & value-added derivatives  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong long-term growth visibility driven by paints scale-up and specialty portfolio, despite near-term pricing pressure

**ELARA CAPITAL: CONSUMER DURABLES VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- LPG supply constraints impacting production  
- Some manufacturers operating at 40–75% capacity  
- Players with alternate fuel sources running near full capacity  

**SECTOR IMPACT**  
- Component inventory at ~7–10 days (tight supply)  
- Compressor segment may see Q4 revenue decline  
- OEMs expected to deliver double-digit YoY growth in Q4  

**MARGINS & DEMAND**  
- Margins likely to decline ~50–150 bps YoY  
- RAC segment to see low–mid double-digit growth  

**PREFERRED PICKS**  
- Voltas  
- LG Electronics  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Mixed  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Supply-side constraints pressure production and margins, but strong demand keeps growth outlook intact

**HDFC BANK: HSBC MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price: ₹1,990 (from ₹1,070)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Chairman resignation seen as transition-related, not governance concern  
- However, may lead to near-term valuation multiple compression  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Strong operating performance expected to offset sentiment impact  
- Core fundamentals remain intact  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Leadership change may weigh on sentiment short-term, but robust fundamentals support long-term outlook

**INDIGO: GOLDMAN SACHS MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy maintained  
- Target Price cut to ₹5,200 (from ₹6,000)  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Supply constraints expected to persist over next few quarters  
- Higher fuel costs (Middle East impact) to weigh on near-term earnings  
- Estimates for FY26–FY28 revised downward  

**SECTOR & STRATEGY VIEW**  
- Focus on cost control and balance sheet strength  
- IndiGo well positioned for industry consolidation  
- Strong track record of capital preservation (similar to COVID phase)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Near-term pressure from fuel and supply issues, but strong fundamentals and consolidation benefits support long-term outlook
A Quick Market Update from 3P_260321_093523.pdf

**CITI: FINANCIALS (CGS 2.0 SCHEME)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- CGS 2.0 offers 80% sovereign guarantee → reduces credit risk & capital burden  
- ₹20,000 Cr scheme size (~20%+ of NBFC-MFI borrowings)  
- Aims to bridge borrowing gaps vs disbursements (Q3FY26)  

**SECTOR IMPACT**  
- Strong liquidity boost for small & mid MFIs  
- Limited direct benefit for large MFIs due to caps  
- AUM-linked allocation ensures broader access, avoids concentration risk  

**FUNDING DYNAMICS**  
- Could enable ~₹8,000 Cr incremental borrowings (entity-level caps)  
- Improves lender confidence & credit flow to microfinance sector  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive (especially for small/mid MFIs)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- CGS 2.0 acts as a key liquidity support measure for MFIs, stabilising funding access while maintaining disciplined risk distribution

**CITI: OIL & GAS (GAS VS OIL DYNAMICS)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Gas value chain faces deeper & more prolonged disruption vs oil  
- Damage to Qatari LNG facility reinforces tight LNG supply outlook  
- JKM LNG prices: ~$27/mmBtu (Q2’26) → $16–17 (H2’26) → ~$13 (CY27)  

**SECTOR VIEW**  
- LNG tightness remains key overhang for gas players (e.g., Petronet)  
- GAIL relatively better positioned within gas ecosystem  
- Oil vs gas divergence critical for investment decisions  

**OIL MARKET OUTLOOK**  
- Brent may stay elevated at $110–120/bbl near term  
- Expected de-escalation in 3–5 weeks → prices cooling to $70–80/bbl by year-end  

**OMC INSIGHT**  
- OMCs near peak stress; valuations at ~0.9–1x P/B  
- BPCL & IOCL relatively more resilient vs HPCL  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative for gas; Mixed for oil & OMCs  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Gas markets face prolonged disruption risk, while oil likely to normalise faster; selective opportunities emerging in OMCs and oil-linked plays

**MORGAN STANLEY: PHARMA (GLP-1 OPPORTUNITY)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- GLP-1 shifting from niche therapy → mass consumer category  
- Generic semaglutide launch driving ~70% price reduction  
- Market size ~₹9,000 Cr (FY23, IQVIA)  

**GROWTH IMPACT**  
- GLP-1 expected to add ~1–2% incremental growth to IPM  
- Strong volume expansion likely with improved affordability  

**COMPETITIVE DYNAMICS**  
- Sun Pharma & Dr Reddy’s well positioned to scale early  
- Initial margins may remain low amid market share capture  
- Tirzepatide ramp-up could further boost growth  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- GLP-1 transition to mass market is a structural growth trigger for pharma, with volume-led expansion outweighing near-term margin pressure

**ADANI PORTS: INVESTEC MAINTAINS BUY**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Buy rating maintained  
- Target Price: ₹1,850  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Vizhinjam port capacity to expand ~2x to 3.7 mn TEUs by Dec  
- Targeting >₹1,000 Cr capex-led growth  
- Strong opportunity in transshipment volumes (import substitution)  

**STRATEGIC DRIVERS**  
- MoU with BPCL for LNG bunkering  
- Capacity expansion across key ports: Mundra, Dhamra, Kattupalli, Kandla & Colombo  
- Strengthening competitive positioning vs global transshipment hubs  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Volume growth supported by capacity expansion & new services  
- Long-term structural growth from India’s rising trade & logistics demand  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- मजबूत expansion pipeline + transshipment opportunity positions Adani Ports for sustained volume and earnings growth

**MORGAN STANLEY: OIL & GAS (INDIA OMCs)**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- Indian fuel retailers facing ~$1.5 bn monthly losses at $107/bbl Brent  
- Crude procurement at Brent-linked pricing with $5–10/bbl premium  
- Strong past profitability (>$8/bbl EBITDA) provides buffer  

**PRICING & POLICY OUTLOOK**  
- Govt likely to absorb part of losses or allow gradual price hikes  
- Fuel price revisions expected post state elections (Apr–May)  
- Recent partial price hikes (~₹2–2.5/litre) cover limited demand (~15%)  

**SECTOR VIEW**  
- Near-term margin pressure for OMCs due to elevated crude  
- Refiners better placed with pricing flexibility  
- Earnings risk: FY27 impact estimated at ~3–5%  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Negative (near-term for OMCs)  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Elevated crude driving losses for OMCs; pricing actions likely delayed but inevitable, with partial relief from government support and gradual hikes

**MORGAN STANLEY: INDIA ENERGY & CHEMICAL VIEW**  

**RATING & TARGET CHANGES**  
- ONGC: Overweight (TP ↑ to ₹363 from ₹299)  
- GAIL: Downgraded to Equal-Weight (TP ↓ to ₹155 from ₹236)  
- Petronet LNG: Downgraded to Equal-Weight (TP ↓ to ₹276 from ₹342)  

**SECTOR VIEW**  
- Higher demand for alternative fuels (coal, diesel) amid energy disruption  
- Positive on upstream oil, coal ecosystem, refiners & fertilisers  
- Gas-based companies face demand & pricing pressure  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAY**  
- Prefer upstream oil producers with integrated refining capabilities  
- Cautious on gas transmission & LNG players due to weak outlook  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive for upstream & coal; Negative for gas players  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Energy shift favours oil & coal segments; gas ecosystem faces structural headwinds in current environment

**L&T: BROKERAGE TARGET SUMMARY**  

**TARGET PRICES**  
- CLSA: ₹4,842  
- JPMorgan: ₹4,570  
- Jefferies: ₹4,500  
- Citi: ₹4,450  
- Goldman Sachs: ₹4,420  

**CONSENSUS VIEW**  
- Average target: ₹4,544  
- Implies ~30%+ upside from current levels  

**KEY MONITORABLES**  
- Stabilisation of Middle East conflict  
- Viability of alternative shipping & logistics routes  
- Ability to maintain execution (“business as usual”)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong institutional confidence with meaningful upside, but dependent on geopolitical stability and execution continuity

**L&T: MACQUARIE MAINTAINS OUTPERFORM**  

**RATING & TARGET**  
- Maintain Outperform  
- Target Price: ₹4,910 (CMP: ₹3,838)  

**OPERATIONAL UPDATE**  
- 95% of project sites continue to operate normally  
- ~5% sites temporarily stalled due to proximity to risk zones (military bases)  
- Stalled sites currently have limited revenue contribution  

**KEY RISKS & OBSERVATIONS**  
- Logistics & supply chain risks flagged amid geopolitical tensions  
- No major operational disruption so far  

**VALUATION & OUTLOOK**  
- Stock corrected ~25% in the last month  
- Macquarie sees significant value at current levels  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Strong execution continuity with limited disruption; sharp correction offers attractive entry opportunity

**GLP-1 IMPACT ON INDIA: JPMORGAN VIEW**  

**KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- GLP-1 not an imminent near-term disruption  
- Estimated ~1% revenue impact by FY28 for F&B companies  
- Global staples already seeing de-rating due to GLP-1 trends  

**CONSUMPTION SHIFT**  
- Changing habits towards healthier consumption  
- Rising preference for low-calorie, protein & fiber-rich products  
- Potential shift in product mix and demand patterns  

**SECTOR IMPACT**  
- Likely impact on Nestle, Britannia, VBL  
- Alcohol & QSR exposure: United Breweries, United Spirits, Jubilant Food  

**STRATEGIC TAKEAWAY**  
- Companies need to adapt portfolios to evolving health trends  
- Innovation in healthier offerings becomes critical  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Gradual structural shift rather than immediate disruption; long-term consumption trends evolving towards healthier choices

**ULTRATECH CEMENT: CLSA MAINTAINS HIGH CONVICTION OUTPERFORM**  

**BROKER VIEW & KEY HIGHLIGHTS**  
- CLSA maintains high conviction outperform rating  
- Middle East conflict raises cost inflation concerns  
- Risks around crude-linked inputs (petcoke, packaging) impacting profitability  

**COST & PRICING DYNAMICS**  
- Estimated cement price hike of 4–5% for $40/T rise in petcoke  
- ~30% increase in packaging costs adds pressure  
- Near-term earnings may remain under stress  

**OUTLOOK**  
- Medium term: Companies likely to retain part of price hikes once cost pressures ease  
- Stock currently at trough valuations on EV/T basis  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Neutral to Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Short-term margin pressure due to cost inflation, but strong pricing power and attractive valuations support medium-term upside

reports.text:
One of our long-time community members just dropped a song — Jaana Tu by VISMRIT 💫
He’s been with us for a while, and this one is straight from the heart…

Let’s support him 🤍
Give it a listen and drop your honest feedback 🎧

https://youtu.be/f3t2Knu_9BY?si=tejYV5Dm04nCt8Xk

**TILAKNAGAR INDUSTRIES (TIL): JM FINANCIAL INITIATION**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- Rating: BUY | CMP ₹441 | TP ₹550 (~25% upside)  
- Valuation: 35x FY28E EPS (discount to peers)  
- Implied ~20x FY28E EV/EBITDA  
- Discount due to lower margins, RoIC & higher leverage  

**CORE THESIS**  
- Legacy business turnaround + premiumisation  
- Transformational Imperial Blue (IB) acquisition  
- Strong volume growth + margin expansion  
- Deleveraging + improving FCF profile  

**LEGACY BUSINESS TURNAROUND**  
- Sales CAGR: ~16.6% (FY20–25)  
- EBITDA: Loss → ₹2,549 mn (FY25)  
- EBITDA margin: ~15–16%  
- Net debt: ₹11 bn → Net cash (FY25)  
- Strong brandy leadership (Mansion House, Courrier Napoleon)  

**IMPERIAL BLUE (IB) ACQUISITION**  
- Deal size: ~₹43.5 bn (from Pernod Ricard)  
- Volume: ~22 mn cases (1.6–1.9x legacy scale)  
- EBITDA margin: ~11–13% (scope to improve)  
- Transforms TIL into Top-5 IMFL player in India  

**PORTFOLIO TRANSFORMATION**  
- Whisky mix: <10% → ~67% post acquisition  
- Geography: South-heavy → Pan-India (50:50 mix)  
- Strong entry into whisky + white spirits  
- Full price ladder from deluxe → premium  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- Legacy: ~11% volume CAGR (FY26–28E)  
- IB revival: 6–8% volume CAGR  
- Combined: ~8–9% volume growth  
- Premiumisation + A&P push + distribution expansion  

**MARGIN EXPANSION**  
- IB margin expansion: +225–350 bps  
- Combined margin uplift: +150–250 bps  
- EBITDA CAGR: ~17% (FY26–28E)  
- Synergies: procurement, logistics, overheads  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK**  
- Revenue: ₹24.8 bn → ₹53.9 bn (FY26–28E)  
- EBITDA: ₹4.1 bn → ₹7.7 bn  
- EPS: ₹9.9 → ₹15.5  
- ROE: improves to ~11% by FY28E  

**LEVERAGE & CASH FLOW**  
- Net Debt/EBITDA:  
  - FY25: Net cash  
  - FY26E: ~5.1x (post acquisition)  
  - FY28E: ~2.0x  
- FCF: Negative FY26 → strong recovery thereafter  
- Low capex → strong deleveraging visibility  

**COMPETITIVE POSITIONING**  
- #3 P&A player Pan-India  
- Strong brandy leadership in South  
- IB → strong entry-level whisky positioning  
- Competes with Radico, Allied, Diageo  

**RISKS**  
- Regulatory (state policies, taxation)  
- IB integration risk  
- Raw material cost volatility (ENA, glass)  
- Brand litigation (Mansion House)  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- Structural transformation story (brandy → pan-India whisky player)  
- IB acquisition is key re-rating trigger  
- Deleveraging + premiumisation → long-term compounding potential

**NTPC GREEN ENERGY (NGEL): HDFC SEC INITIATION**  

**VALUATION & TARGET**  
- Rating: BUY | CMP ₹99 | TP ₹121 (~22% upside)  
- Valuation: 14.5x FY29E EV/EBITDA (discounted @12%)  
- Premium justified by scale, execution visibility & low-cost debt  
- REIT-like model: long-term PPAs + stable cash flows  

**BUSINESS POSITIONING**  
- Renewable arm of NTPC Ltd  
- Key vehicle for 60 GW RE target by FY32  
- Presence across 9 states via subsidiaries & JVs  
- Expanding into BESS, green hydrogen, PSP & green chemicals  

**CAPACITY & PIPELINE**  
- Current capacity: ~8 GW (solar + wind)  
- Contracted/awarded: 15.5 GW (FY29 visibility)  
- Pipeline: 9.1 GW  
- MoUs/JVs: ~37 GW (long-term optionality)  
- Land bank: ~2.5 lakh acres (supports >50 GW capacity)  

**BESS & STORAGE PLAY**  
- Total BESS pipeline: >13,000 MWh  
- Includes: TBCB wins, co-located solar, thermal integration  
- Pilot CO₂-based storage (160 MWh)  
- Strong positioning in RTC (round-the-clock power)  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
- India power demand CAGR ~6.6% (FY26–35)  
- Solar demand ~35 GW/year  
- Data centers + electrification boosting demand  
- Hybrid RE + PSP enabling firm power supply  

**COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE**  
- NTPC legacy → execution & financing strength  
- ~100 bps lower cost of debt vs peers  
- Strong PPA tie-ups + transmission connectivity  
- Scale advantage → better procurement & IRR  

**FINANCIAL OUTLOOK (FY26–29E)**  
- Revenue/EBITDA CAGR: ~82%  
- EBITDA margin: ~88% (high due to RE model)  
- Capacity: 2.6 GW → 27.4 GW (FY23–FY29E)  
- OCF: ~₹379 bn vs capex ₹1.2 tn  

**CAPEX & FUNDING**  
- Total capex: ~₹2.4 tn till FY32  
- Funding mix: 60–75% debt, balance equity  
- Additional equity likely beyond ~25 GW expansion  

**PORTFOLIO & PPA PROFILE**  
- 25-year PPAs across portfolio  
- Residual PPA life: ~21 years  
- Counterparties: SECI, DISCOMs, PSUs  
- Solar-heavy mix shifting to hybrid + wind  

**RISKS**  
- High leverage during capex phase  
- Tariff compression (₹2.7–3/kWh vs earlier ₹3.3–4.5)  
- Execution risk in large-scale rollout  
- Equity dilution for future expansion  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  
- Positive  

**OVERALL TAKEAWAY**  
- One of India’s most scalable RE platforms  
- Strong visibility via PPAs + land + pipeline  
- Long-term compounding story driven by energy transition

**HDFC Bank – Broker Note: Key Data**

- **Rating**: BUY  
- **CMP**: Not specified in text, target calibrated off it  
- **Target Price**: ₹1,120  
- **Upside**: ~40%  
- **Valuation basis**: around **1.7x forward adjusted book value (ABV)**, viewed as historically low with limited downside  

**Governance Event & Interim Arrangement**  
- **Part‑time Chairman Atanu Chakraborty resigned abruptly**, citing **“happenings and practices within the bank” not aligned with his personal values and ethics**, without sharing specifics with the Board despite asked  
- **RBI approved Keki Mistry** as **interim part‑time chairman for 3 months**; his comments plus an RBI statement on governance/financial position taken as **reassuring**  

**Management & Board Stability**  
- Seen multiple **senior exits / superannuation** and **regulatory action at Dubai branch**  
- Stability of **MD & CEO role** (term ends Oct‑26) and **overall senior management** flagged as critical  
- **NRC will soon meet on MD & CEO reappointment**; bank emphasises **no power struggle** in the Board or executive team; recent key decisions **unanimous**  

**Key Messages from Investor Call**  
- Board stresses **strong corporate governance**, **no known regulatory lapses**, and that the **resignation letter’s wording “defies logic”**  
- Issue interpreted as **interpersonal/human‑relationship friction**, not **systemic governance failure**  
- **No operational issues** acknowledged  

**Financial Snapshot (FY25–28E)**  
- **NII**: ₹1,227bn → ₹1,773bn  
- **Net profit**: ₹674bn → ₹1,027bn  
- **RoA**: 1.8% → 2.0%  
- **RoE**: 14.1% → 15.2%  
- **Asset quality (FY25–28)**:  
  - **GNPA**: 1.2–1.4%  
  - **NNPA**: 0.3–0.4%  
  - **CET‑1**: 17–17.2%  

**Growth & Rerating Thesis**  
- Expect **higher‑than‑system deposit growth (6–7% QoQ)** and **loan growth broadly in line with system (4–5% QoQ)** near term  
- Stock trading about **2 standard deviations below long‑term P/ABV average**  
- Broker argues that **stable execution on deposits/loans plus management continuity** could drive **valuation rerating**

**DAM Capital Note – Key Highlights (Macro & Sector)**

**Macro & Commodities (Strait of Hormuz shock)**  
- Closure hits **~20% of global LNG seaborne trade**, **~32% of LPG seaborne exports** and **~10% of seaborne oil**  
- India is highly dependent on **Strait of Hormuz (SOH)** for **LNG, LPG and oil**  
- For India: each **1 USD/bbl above budgeted 70 USD/bbl** adds **~₹100bn to fiscal deficit**  
- Sustained **85–90+ USD/bbl crude** can push **CAD to 1.5–2% of GDP in Q1 FY27**, **CPI >4.5%** and **INR toward 95**  

**Oil Availability vs Product Bottlenecks**  
- **Crude and transport fuels (gasoline, diesel, ATF)** are “manageable” via **inventories, IEA release and Russian supply**  
- Real bottlenecks: **refined products, LNG, LPG, NGLs, methanol, sulphur, helium and aluminium** with **sharp price risk**  

**India – Availability & Pricing**  
- Roughly **okay on crude and transport fuels** thanks to domestic refining and diversified sourcing; **LPG and bitumen** most stressed  
  - LPG inventory ~15 days  
  - Long lead times from US  
- **Petrol/diesel pass‑through politically constrained** (election‑heavy year):  
  - OMCs initially absorb **marketing losses**  
  - **Excise cuts likely** if crude stays elevated  
- **External‑sector headwinds**:  
  - Higher **freight/insurance (potential 2x)**  
  - Longer routes via **Cape of Good Hope**  
  - Around **38% of India’s remittances** at risk from Gulf disruptions  

**Big Macro Conclusions**  
- **Near term**: macro fundamentals hold; stress appears first in **CAD, INR and subsidies** (oil, fertiliser)  
- **If war lasts >1 quarter with crude >100 USD/bbl**:  
  - **Inflation risk (CPI >4.5%)**  
  - **Pause in rate cuts**  
  - **Fiscal deficit 10–20 bps wider**  
  - **GDP growth downgrade (FY27 to ~6.8% vs 7%)**  

**Sector Winners (India – relative positives)**  
- **Upstream O&G (ONGC, Oil India)**:  
  - **~11–12% EBITDA uplift per 5 USD/bbl**  
  - Lower perceived **windfall‑tax risk** under ORDA framework  
- **RIL (Refining, petchem)**:  
  - **~2.5% EBITDA boost per 1 USD/bbl GRM gain**  
  - Spot GRMs >25 USD/bbl vs normal ~8 USD/bbl; petchem also improving  
- **Sugar**:  
  - Higher crude → **more Brazilian ethanol diversion** → **tighter global sugar**  
  - Better **prices and export opportunity** (0.6–1 MT vs 0.3 MT already exported)  
  - **India ethanol‑blending push** may accelerate  
- **Select agro‑input names** (fertilisers, sulphur‑linked) benefit from **inventory gains** and **better pricing** if disruption persists  

**Sector Losers (India – clear negatives)**  
- **OMCs (IOCL, BPCL, HPCL) & CGDs (IGL, MGL, GUJGAS)**:  
  - **~30–50% EBITDA hit per 5 USD/bbl** (no retail price hike)  
  - **CGDs**: lose **~₹2/scm of EBITDA (15–30%) without pass‑through**  
- **Airlines**:  
  - **ATF is 35–40% of cost**; **10% ATF rise cuts EBITDA ~15%**  
  - Also hit by **ME airspace closures and rerouting of long‑haul**  
- **Infra**:  
  - Higher oil squeezes fiscal space and **Middle East order flows**  
  - Risk to India’s **>₹12tn capex plan** if crude averages ~90 USD/bbl (~₹2tn fiscal hit)  
- **Cement**:  
  - No ME fuel sourcing, but **10% rise in petcoke/coal cuts EBITDA/t by ~8% (~₹130/t)**  
  - Polypropylene bag costs add another **~₹80/t**  
- **Tiles & bathware / wood panels**:  
  - Gas and resin cost inflation → **70–150 bps margin risk** for tiles, **30–60 bps** for wood panels on 10% energy/RM rise  
  - Added **volume pressure** from weak demand  

**Mixed / Time‑Dependent Impacts (India)**  
- **Fertilisers**:  
  - Near‑term **input‑cost/logistics shock**; not an immediate availability crisis  
  - **DAP** most exposed via imports, **urea** on gas/curtailment  
  - **SSP and NPK** better placed but face **sulphur/ammonia/phosphate cost risk**  
- **Chemicals**:  
  - Near‑term **inventory gains**, then **refilling at higher RM and freight costs**  
  - Most exposed chains: **methanol, ammonia, crude‑linked petrochemicals** (ethylene, propylene, benzene, toluene)  
- **FMCG**:  
  - **Crude‑linked packaging (PET, HDPE, laminates
